items.title: Mark McGowan's right-hand man parachuted into plum cabinet role

children.content: New Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli, the former chief of staff to both Mark McGowan and Roger Cook, will be parachuted into cabinet to replace Paul Papalia, the ABC understands.

gallery.altText: Daniel Pastorelli looks into the distance while standing outside a building.

thumbnail.altText: Daniel Pastorelli looks into the distance while standing outside a building.

items.title: Record fine for gold miner thrusts tiny bat into spotlight

image.altText: A small bat is held with two hands with its wings spread apart.

children.content: A Western Australian gold miner has been hit with a fine of more than $100,000 for failing to protect a vulnerable bat species.

items.title: 'We'll sell his body parts': AI extortionists target missing man's parents

children.content: Scammers have sent the parents of a missing West Australian man an AI-generated image of their son and demanded thousands of dollars in exchange for his safety.

image.altText: A man with short dark hair and a close beard wearing a khaki green t-shirt sitting outside a playground.

items.title: Water in the air an 'untapped water resource' until now

children.content: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

image.altText: A woman stands with solar panels behind her, she is looking up.

items.title: Can Fremantle keep rolling to the minor premiership?

children.content: Fremantle's 14-game winning streak ended with a loss to GWS, but what are the keys for the Dockers to keep going and set up a tilt at their first flag? Cody Atkinson and Sean Lawson investigate.

items.title: AFL's best two teams ready for titanic battle under Perth lights

children.content: Fremantle take on Sydney at Perth Stadium tonight, in a match boasting storylines as compelling as early-era Melrose Place. Who will emerge from the clash as premiership favourites?

image.altText: Luke Jackson celebrates an AFL goal with a Dockers teammate.

children.content: Rents are soaring in several capital cities as low vacancy rates collide with rising interest rates and population growth.

items.title: Telstra solves 'software defect' after hundreds of failed Triple Zero calls

children.content: Telstra has implemented a fresh "solution" to resolve a secondary software defect that left hundreds of customers unable to make Triple Zero calls during a nationwide network outage.

articles.title: SA police investigating possible Telstra outage death — as it happened

articles.title: Why a telco outage has stopped some trains but not others

children.content: Trains relying on the 4G network of freight rail track operator ARTC went down amid the Telstra failure.

articles.title: 'Shouldn't occur': Change demanded after Telstra outage

children.content: Advocates have demanded changes to how telecommunication companies are regulated and held accountable for outages after Telstra became the latest telco to suffer a large-scale network failure.

items.title: Police call for help to handle 'disgusting' Facebook comments

children.content: The WA Police commissioner writes to Meta to ask for assistance with Facebook moderation after a number of derogatory comments are left on social media pages.

image.altText: A medium close-up shot of WA Police Commissioner Col Blanch in uniform.

items.title: Foreign investor snub of divestment order 'extraordinary', says expert

children.content: China-linked investors in a Western Australia rare earths company have yet to comply with federal Treasurer Jim Chalmers's latest order to sell their shares.

image.altText: A remote mining camp in the outback

items.title: Growers push to overturn ban on ancient 'superfood'

children.content: Farmers are appealing against a federal ban on the sale of
Moringa oleifera
as a food after a regulatory body determined there was insufficient evidence to prove its safety.

items.title: SA confirms second H5 bird flu case, two more suspected in Perth and WA

items.title: Swimming Australia fights 'scary' fake news in world-first initiative
